text.title: RAG Pipeline Design — Worked Example
text.summary: Worked example: Advanced RAG over support KB with Qdrant + Cohere Rerank + Ragas eval gate.

example.title: Production rag-pipeline.yaml
example.code:
tier: advanced
embedding: model: voyage-3-large dim: 1024 pin_version: "2026-04"
vector_db: kind: qdrant connection: host: qdrant.internal port: 6333 collection: support-kb multi_tenant: true required_filter: tenant_id
chunking: strategy: recursive size: 512 overlap: 50 document_overrides: legal: { strategy: paragraph, size: 400 } code-docs: { strategy: header-based, size: 512 }
retrieval: mode: hybrid top_k_first_stage: 20 fusion: rrf weights: vector: 0.65 bm25: 0.35
reranker: kind: cohere model: rerank-multilingual-v3.0 top_n: 5
prompt: citation_required: true fallback_phrase: "I don't have enough information to answer this with confidence."
eval_gate: framework: ragas labelled_set_path: evals/support-kb-100.jsonl thresholds: faithfulness: ">= 0.90" context_recall: ">= 0.85" answer_relevance: ">= 0.80"
telemetry: log_query_embedding: true log_retrieved_ids: true log_scores: true emit_to: drift-alerts
example.note: Advanced tier (r1), hybrid retrieval (r2), 512 / 50 chunks with document overrides (r3), Cohere Reranker (r4), citation enforcement (r5), Ragas gate (r6).

example.title: Anti-example caught by the schema validator
example.code: tier: agentic # over-tier for FAQ; r1 violation flagged at review retrieval: { mode: vector } # r2 violation: no BM25 # reranker missing # r4 violation prompt: { citation_required: false } # r5 violation # eval_gate missing # r6 violation
example.note: Validator returns 5 violations referencing r1, r2, r4, r5, r6 — every rule shows up as a concrete schema rejection.
